title: The Francophone Immigration Support Program: Expected outcomes - Canada.ca
content:
To receive funding, applicants must demonstrate how their project will contribute to at least one of the following Francophone Immigration Support Program (FISP) immediate outcomes:
- Francophone partners are more involved in promoting Francophone minority communities (FMCs) identifying, supporting and recruiting French-speaking candidates
- promoting Francophone minority communities (FMCs)
- identifying, supporting and recruiting French-speaking candidates
- French-speaking candidates are better informed of immigration opportunities in FMCs
- French-speaking temporary residents are better equipped to apply for programs leading to permanent
        immigration
- FISP activities are supported by evidence-based data
The immediate outcomes of the projects should aim to impact the following intermediate and long-term outcomes:
Intermediate outcome
Increase in the number of admissions of French-speaking candidates resulting, in whole or in part, from eligible
    activities and projects under the FISP, for both the permanent residence and the temporary residence programs.
Foster demographic growth and economic development in FMCs, both through the selection of French-speaking permanent
    residents and the admission of French-speaking temporary residents.
